query: Ingest this: it's a full textbook (Sedgewick's Algorithms, 4th edition), not a single paper.
expected_behavior: Recognizes source_type: book and switches to the book-specific extraction approach rather than the default single-thesis paper flow
Produces a structure map (chapter/section outline) as the skeleton of the source page
Extracts core definitions, key methods/algorithms, and important principles as separate concept pages rather than one monolithic page
Explicitly does NOT attempt to summarize every chapter — instead surfaces the ~10-20 most central ideas per the skill's stated depth rule
Captures a prerequisite chain via requires: links between concept pages where the book implies one topic depends on another
